vocab.example: I check the mirror purely for my aesthetic presentation, making sure my attire is coordinated before a meeting.

vocab.meaning: tầm nhìn ngoại vi (liếc mắt qua gương)
vocab.example: I often catch my reflection in my peripheral vision when passing hallway mirrors, though I rarely stop to stare.

vocab.meaning: tự làm đẹp, chăm chút bản thân trước gương
vocab.example: I’m not the type of person to spend hours preening myself in front of the glass every morning.

vocab.meaning: các đường nét đối xứng trên khuôn mặt
vocab.example: Sometimes I find myself analyzing the symmetrical features of my face when I’m bored in the bathroom.

vocab.meaning: cái nhìn vô tình/không cố ý
vocab.example: Most of the time, it’s just an inadvertent glance while I’m washing my hands rather than an intentional look.
Mirrors provide essential visual feedback when I’m trying on new clothes to see how they actually drape on my frame.
I try to avoid excessive mirror-gazing because I don't want to succumb to superficial vanity.

vocab.meaning: ánh sáng tôn dáng/làm đẹp gương mặt
vocab.example: In flattering lighting, I might spend a bit more time looking at myself, as it definitely improves my mood.

vocab.meaning: nhận thức về hình ảnh bản thân
vocab.example: Frequent use of mirrors can actually distort one's self-image perception if they become too fixated on minor flaws.
I occasionally use full-length mirrors to adjust my posture and ensure I’m standing up straight.
